steps.title: Identify the poem's structural turn
steps.content: Lines 1–8 are pure description: sensory details and adjectives applied to the cypress (“slim and still,” “Sensitive,” “Exquisite,” “pointing upwards”). Line 9 marks an abrupt shift: “Why, beautiful still finger, are you black? And why are you pointing upwards?” The poem moves from portraying what the speaker sees to asking about what it means.

steps.title: Match the structure to the correct interpretation
steps.content: Choice B states the speaker “describes a distinctive sight in nature, then ponders what meaning to attribute to that sight”—exactly the move from visual description to asking about symbolic significance. A wrongly claims the speaker questions her descriptive accuracy; C invents human behavior absent from the poem; D misidentifies the questioning as being about emotional influence. That matches choice B — B is correct.
